LHX.sentence: L3Harris ticked up slightly, but its profit margins are running thinner than its bigger defense rivals, which is the main thing weighing on it even though it's otherwise been beaten down quite a bit.

ASML.sentence: ASML has been climbing for weeks now and is stretched a bit above its usual trend, which is normal for the only company on earth that makes the machines used to print the most advanced computer chips.

LIN.sentence: Linde is flat and quiet — the industrial gas business runs on steady long-term contracts, and there's nothing dramatic happening this week.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft has had a rough slide and is well below where it's traded most of the year, which is unusual for them — the underlying cloud and AI story hasn't changed, the stock just got beaten down.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is flat on the day and looks unusually cheap versus its own history, mostly because lab and biotech spending is in a slow patch that tends to recover over time.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is steady — its pipelines act like toll roads with a reliable dividend, though that dividend isn't paying much more than safe government bonds right now, so it's looking a touch expensive.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow is flat today and has gotten much cheaper relative to its past, even as big companies and the government keep signing on for its workflow-automation and AI assistant tools.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore slipped a bit today and has lagged other copper miners lately, which leaves it some room to catch up if metals prices cooperate.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin has fallen a long way and is sitting just above its lowest point of the past year, so it's bruised but barely budged today.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is unchanged — softer soda sales in Mexico, partly due to a new tax and cautious shoppers, are being made up for by stronger growth in South America.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is flat today, but oil prices have been sliding for several days and are now at their lowest since February, which pressures this Brazilian oil giant and its big dividends.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish company that hauls containers across the oceans, barely moved today and is trading below what its fleet of ships is worth — a cautious price for a business that mirrors global trade.
